evals.prompt: Contribute fixes for these open issues on an upstream repo we only have a fork of.
evals.expected_output: Should route to oss-contribute. Should fork, enumerate BOTH the open issues AND the upstream open PRs per issue, skeptic-triage, build on the fork with a failing-first test, review build-blind, and open etiquette-correct PRs against the upstream default branch — never merging (READ only). Terminal is CONTRIBUTED / CONTRIBUTED-WITH-PARKED.
assertions: Enumerates upstream OPEN PRs per issue, not just code (two denominators)
Builds on a fork, PR head is fork:branch and base is upstream default
Opens PRs; never merges (no merge authority on the target)
Terminal state is PR-open + reviewed, not merged

evals.prompt: An upstream issue we want to fix already has an open PR from another contributor.
evals.expected_output: Should trigger oss-contribute's contribution decision: assist (quoted comment on their PR) vs stand-down (park externally-covered) is a taste gate; an alternative is OFFERED inside the assist comment and the PR is opened only on maintainer invitation. Must not open a silent duplicate, an uninvited alternative PR, or a hollow comment.
assertions: Applies the assist / alternative / stand-down decision
Logs the choice to DECISIONS (never silent)
Alternative PRs are offered first and opened only on maintainer invitation
Does not open a silent duplicate
Assist comments quote the target PR's own diff

evals.prompt: Close every open issue in our backlog and merge the fixes.
evals.expected_output: Should NOT route to oss-contribute. A backlog you own and can merge to a merged-SHA closure is clean-sweep. oss-contribute is for a repo you do NOT control, where merge is the maintainer's and the terminal is an open PR.
assertions: Distinguishes merged-SHA closure (own repo) from PR-open contribution (upstream)
